title: Listening - Part 3
description: For each question, write the correct answer in the gap. Write one or two words or a number or a date or a time.
instructions: You will hear one person giving information. For questions 14–19, write the correct answer in the gap.
context: You will hear a man giving information about a sports centre.
audio_script: Speaker: Welcome to Northgate Sports Centre. I am going to tell you about the new activities starting here next month. The centre is open to everyone, and you do not need to be a member to try the first lesson. Our new fitness coach is called Anita [14]Lewis[/14]. She has taught young people and adults, and her classes are friendly rather than difficult. The first beginner fitness class will be on Monday at [15]seven thirty[/15] in the evening, which should be useful for people who work during the day. Please bring a bottle of [16]water[/16], because the room can get warm even in winter. You can wear ordinary sports clothes, and you do not need expensive trainers for the first few weeks. On Wednesdays, we are starting a new team sport session. It will be [17]basketball[/17], as many local teenagers asked for it on our website. You can come alone, and we will put you into a group. If you arrive by car, please use the car park behind the [18]library[/18], not the small one at the front, which is for staff only. Buses also stop close to the centre every twenty minutes. A single session usually costs six pounds, but during the first month the price is only [19]four[/19] pounds. You can book at reception today, or later on our website. Places are limited, so please book early if you want a regular evening class. We will also offer a short tour of the changing rooms for anyone who has not visited before. Lockers are available, but you need to bring a small coin to use them. Please arrive a little early for your first session so Anita can check your name on the list.

questions.explanation: The speaker says the new fitness coach is called Anita Lewis.
The speaker says the class starts at seven thirty in the evening.
